name: gunicorn
explanation: The Gunicorn check does not probe the network at all. It uses `psutil.process_iter` on the host where the Agent runs to find a master process whose cmdline matches `gunicorn: master [<proc_name>]`. The required `proc_name` is application-specific (it's the value the user passed to `gunicorn --name`) and is set by the application owner; it cannot be derived from the wire or from any well-known list. Discovery would require enumerating every gunicorn master process on every host the Agent can see and then deciding which app names matter — this is process-name discovery, not credential or endpoint discovery, and is out of scope for generic auto-config. Note `setproctitle` must also be installed in the app's Python env for the master title pattern to appear at all.
notes: Discovery here is local-process based, not network. Even if `proc_name` could be discovered by scanning host processes, picking which gunicorn apps to monitor is a user-policy question the Agent cannot answer.
